nodes.label: AI as creative brainstorming partner for tailored activities
nodes.label_negative: AI as generic advice giver lacking specificity
nodes.grounding_span: using AI, i can find new ideas that are specifically tailored to each child

nodes.label: AI as administrative assistant for routine tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unable to handle personalized paperwork
nodes.grounding_span: I also like to use AI for my administrative tasks, such as email mockups and lesson plans

nodes.label: AI as jumping-off point for human refinement
nodes.label_negative: AI as complete solution without human input
nodes.grounding_span: I find that I can refine better than I can design. So, if Im stuck on an idea, i use AI as sort of my jumping off point

nodes.label: AI responsiveness to detailed context
nodes.label_negative: AI inability to adapt when given insufficient information
nodes.grounding_span: i realize now that i was not giving the chatbot enough information but even when i would point out an error or repetition it did not seem to recognize that

nodes.label: AI as tool for worker support and connection
nodes.label_negative: AI as irrelevant to emotional needs of workers
nodes.grounding_span: i would hope it could be for this [creating platforms to help people in my profession connect with each other]

nodes.label: AI reliability in training contexts
nodes.label_negative: AI inconsistency and inaccuracy in training
nodes.grounding_span: when trying to use my Ai for working training it was VERY hit or miss. somethings it got absolutely incorrect while other times it gave me great insight

nodes.label: Personalized care and connection with children
nodes.grounding_span: the connections you foster with the kids, and i believe that taking the time to plan these thoughtful activities for/with them is a great way to make those connections

nodes.label: Efficiency in administrative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: i do wish AI could handle some of the more tiring administrative tasks

nodes.label: Worker well-being and peer support
nodes.grounding_span: working with foster children is not easy... there are not a lot of resources for us to get help or support

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for creative inspiration
nodes.grounding_span: using AI, i can find new ideas that are specifically tailored to each child

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of specificity in behavioral advice
nodes.grounding_span: The AI kept giving me very general pieces of advice, things i had already tried and knew were not the right approach

nodes.label: Wish for AI to handle more administrative burden
nodes.grounding_span: i do wish AI could handle some of the more tiring administrative tasks

nodes.label: Hope for AI to support worker networks
nodes.grounding_span: i would hope it could be for this [creating platforms to help people in my profession connect with each other]

nodes.label: Mixed experience with AI in training
nodes.grounding_span: when trying to use my Ai for working training it was VERY hit or miss

nodes.label: Iterative refinement — uses AI as base then personalizes
nodes.grounding_span: I find that I can refine better than I can design. So, if Im stuck on an idea, i use AI as sort of my jumping off point

nodes.label: Privacy-first approach — describes tasks abstractly to protect client info
nodes.grounding_span: My main priority is always privacy when it comes to the children
